pi_items.source: ISO 639-1 standard language code for French
Task metadata - standard English term for Antenatal Care first visit
Medical French terminology: CPN=Consultation Prénatale (verified via WHO, MSF, and medical literature); 1ère visite is correct French ordinal form
Medical French terminology; 2ème visite is correct ordinal form
Medical French terminology; 3ème visite is correct ordinal form
Medical French terminology; 4ème visite is correct ordinal form
Task metadata - standard English term (IPT=Intermittent Preventive Treatment)
Medical French terminology: TPI=Traitement Préventif Intermittent (verified via WHO publications and medical literature); 1ère dose is correct French form
Verified through medical sources (MSF Medical Guidelines, Reverso Context, academic journals) confirming CPN as standard French abbreviation for antenatal/prenatal consultation
Verified through WHO publications and medical literature for malaria prevention in pregnancy
Verified through academic literature (ACM, WHO, CDC publications) confirming Sierra Leone as one of first sub-Saharan African countries to implement DHIS2 (2008-2009)

Setup script explicitly states (line 83): 'This is official real-world demo data from the DHIS2 project.' Downloaded from https://databases.dhis2.org/sierra-leone/2.40/dhis2-db-sierra-leone.sql.gz (HTTP 200 confirmed)
Verified through DHIS2 official documentation (docs.dhis2.org). Export script at line 87 filters for translations with locale='fr' and property='NAME'
Specified in task.json and confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 38)
Standard DHIS2 demo instance default credentials (setup_task.sh line 13)

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified and accurate. The task uses the official DHIS2 Sierra Leone demo database (real data, not synthetic) to demonstrate adding French translations for antenatal care (CPN) and malaria prevention (TPI) data elements. CPN (Consultation Prénatale) and TPI (Traitement Préventif Intermittent) are authentic medical abbreviations verified through WHO publications and medical literature. All five French translations (CPN 1ère visite, CPN 2ème visite, CPN 3ème visite, CPN 4ème visite, CPN TPI 1ère dose) are linguistically correct and medically accurate. The task operates on a DHIS2 instance with authentic API endpoints and translation structure matching official DHIS2 API documentation.
